response.justification: Sarah Smith has extensive experience as a software developer with a strong background in C++ (used at National Instruments), multi-threaded systems, and high-performance applications. However, her recent work has been heavily focused on web and mobile technologies (TypeScript, Node.js, React) rather than C++ or industrial automation. Her lack of explicit mention of secure development lifecycle (SDL), IEC 62443 standards, or cloud platforms like Azure raises concerns about her direct fit for this role.
strengths: Strong C++ experience (used in LabWindows/CVI at National Instruments)
Experience with high-performance, low-latency systems (e.g., micro-services at Dunnhumby)
Proven track record in software architecture, testing (TDD), and team leadership
concerns: Limited recent C++ development (last clear C++ role was 2014-2015)
No explicit mention of industrial cybersecurity (IEC 62443) or SDL practices
Most recent work is in web/mobile (Node.js, TypeScript), not aligned with core C++/industrial automation requirements

key_factors.factor: Relevance of recent work to industrial automation/C++
